resources.string: Tap the button below to check whether your DNS queries are leaking outside your VPN or private DNS configuration.
Your DNS queries are being handled by the expected resolvers. No leak was detected.
Your DNS queries are being sent to ISP or unexpected resolvers, even though a VPN is active. This means your browsing activity may be visible to your ISP.

When you use a VPN, all your internet traffic — including DNS queries — should be routed through the VPN tunnel. A DNS leak occurs when your device sends DNS queries directly to your ISP\'s DNS servers instead, revealing the websites you visit even though your other traffic is encrypted.
Open Settings → Network & internet → Private DNS
Set to a private DNS provider hostname (e.g. dns.google, one.one.one.one, or dns.quad9.net)
If using a VPN, ensure its DNS leak protection setting is enabled
Re-run this test to verify the fix

DNS leak tests are most meaningful when a VPN is active. Without a VPN, your DNS queries go through your configured DNS servers, which is expected. The resolvers shown below are what your device is currently using.
